oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Understanding metaphylaxis practices for cattle at uncertain risk for bovine respiratory disease across U.S. feedlots: A survey of veterinary consultants and feedlot managers
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Horton, V.
Hanthorn, C.
Thackrah, A.
Renter, D.
Cernicchiaro, N.
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Bovine respiratory disease (BRD) is the most significant disease affecting feedlot cattle in the United States. Metaphylaxis, the targeted administration of antimicrobials to high-risk popula­tions, has proven to effectively reduce morbidity and mortality; however, concerns about antimicrobial resistance warrant its judicious use. The decision of whether to use metaphylaxis in cattle at risk for BRD poses a particular challenge when disease risk classification and cost-benefits are uncertain. Our objec­tive was to collect and analyze qualitative and quantitative data used by veterinarians and feedlot managers across the U.S. when determining metaphylaxis use for cattle cohorts at uncer­tain risk of developing BRD.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 264
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
